name: Koko Crater Railway
tagline: 1,048 railway ties straight up a crater — the most brutal 1.6-mile workout on the island.
parking: Free parking at Koko Head District Park
hours: Open 24 hours (no gates; parking lot at Koko Head District Park always accessible)
bestTime: Pre-dawn start (bring headlamp) — avoid midday heat at all costs
talkStory: The 1,048 railroad ties were laid during WWII as a tramway to haul supplies and personnel to a radar tracking station at the summit — the military needed eyes on the ocean approaches after Pearl Harbor. The ties were originally fitted with rails and a cable car system. The notorious bridge section near the top spans a gap where the original trestle crossed a ravine. Inside the crater at the summit sits a forgotten botanical garden planted in the 1950s with plumeria, cacti, and native dryland species. Locals do this as a pre-dawn workout — arrive with a headlamp.
